title: senecio doria subsp. laderoi
intro: Senecio doria subsp. laderoi, also known as the Canary Islands ragwort or Canary ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Senecio doria subsp. laderoi include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and soothe sore throats. Traditionally, this plant has been prepared as an infusion or a decoction, and its leaves can be used to make a tea, which is said to aid in the relief of respiratory symptoms.
